title: How to run container without default gateway when network use macvlan
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
If there is a way to run container without default gateway, when network use macvlan. There is a scenario, if I want to create a container that only can be accessed by the same subnet, I would create macvlan network without gateway, but it would add default gateway (the first ip of the subnet) after run container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run "podman network create -d macvlan --subnet=192.168.1.0/24 -o parent=ens192 -o mode=bridge ens192"

2. Run "podman run -itd --name=test --network=ens192 <image_id> /bin/bash"

3. Run "podman exec -it <container_id> /bin/bash"
4. Run "ip route"

**Describe the results you received:**
bash-4.2# ip route
default via 192.168.1.1 dev eth0
192.168.1.0/24 dev eth0 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.1.5

**Describe the results you expected:**
I hope it wouldn't add the default gateway as following:
bash-4.2# ip route
192.168.1.0/24 dev eth0 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.1.5

If we only set ip address on redhat "ip addr add 192.168.1.5/24 dev ens192", it wouldn't add default gateway. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: What podman version do you use? Do you use CNI or netavark? If CNI, how does your config file looks like?
You can remove the default route `"dst": "0.0.0.0/0"` and the gateway from this config.
@mheon I think we need a way to do the same for netavark.

If config could pass additional property or empty route array then netavark could avoid adding a default route and I guess hopefully this is only needed for `macvlan` cause `bridge` might always need a default route.
Bridge already supports internal via `--internal` I guess we should use the same for macvlan.
@Luap99 Sure, if we could use `--internal` then its a small patch on `netavark`. I'll try this out.
This will also require patches in libnetwork. I think we error right now when you use --internal and macvlan, and we also have to fix it for CNI as well.
@Luap99 , it work as expected after removing the route and gateway in cni file. thanks.
If CLI validation is removed from `podman` for `--internal` and `macvlan` following PR enables this feature: https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/246

Note that this is not safe when you run a privileged container since it can always add the default route back. For bridge networks we block routing so this secure even for privileged containers. I don't think we can do the same for macvlan, we should not change the host macvlan device to block routing because this could break many other things.

@Luap99 , do you mean podman wouldn't fix it when create macvlan? I use the latest code to build, and it show macvlan don't support internal as following:
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman network create -d macvlan -o parent=ens161 --subnet=192.168.1.0/24 --internal ens161
Error: internal is not supported with macvlan
[root@centos8 bin]#
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.17.7
Git Commit:   c39dffe83db9fa3cfa6897b971956821f1bbcce2
Built:        Tue Mar  1 14:41:51 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@centos8 bin]#
